mechanism_narrative: Higher retail sales indicate robust consumer spending, directly driving corporate revenues and earnings. This growing demand feeds into higher stock valuations over short horizons, producing positive forward returns.
failure_condition: If RSAFS increases month-over-month are followed by negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances over a rolling 2-year window, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate past a critical threshold signals ongoing labor market deterioration, eroding consumer confidence and spending power. This negative demand shock depresses corporate profits and stock prices.
failure_condition: If UNRATE above 5.5% is followed by positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of occurrences over any 3-year period, hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is low relative to inflation (negative real rate), monetary policy is highly accommodative, reducing the discount rate on equity cash flows and boosting stock prices. The signal depends on the joint level of both rates.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < year-over-year change in CPIAUCSL (negative real rate)
failure_condition: If negative real rates are not followed by positive 60-day returns in at least 55% of quarterly observations over a 5-year window, hypothesis is refuted.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation environment (CPI year-over-year below 2%), moderate increases in inflation reflect strengthening demand without immediate overheating fears. This supports profit margins and stock valuations, leading to positive forward returns.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL rises while CPI YoY is below 2% and is followed by negative 20-day returns in more than 40% of months over a rolling 3-year period, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production signals contracting manufacturing activity and broader economic slowdown. This reduces corporate earnings expectations and drags down stock prices over the ensuing quarter.
failure_condition: If an INDPRO monthly decline of 0.5%+ is not followed by negative 20-day returns in at least 60% of instances over a 3-year period, hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening labor market, as evidenced by rising nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment, boosts household income and consumption. This positive demand signal lifts corporate earnings and stock prices in the short term.
failure_condition: If simultaneous increase in PAYEMS and decrease in UNRATE is followed by negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of cases over a 2-year rolling window, hypothesis is refuted.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP reflects broad-based economic expansion, growing corporate earnings and investor confidence. This fundamental strength supports stock prices over quarterly holding periods.
failure_condition: If sequential GDPC1 increases are followed by negative 20-day returns in more than 40% of quarters over a 10-year period, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts below the 1 million-unit threshold signals a deterioration in residential investment and construction sector weakness, which historically precedes broader economic downturns and negative stock market returns.
failure_condition: If HOUST below 1000 is followed by positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of quarters over a 5-year period, hypothesis fails.
